def: s.m. He-goat ( = bakrā); ram. tup.
s.m. He-goat ( = bok, q.v.); basket or leather bucket (generally of goat's hide) for throwing water to a higher elevation.
s.m. Name of a town and country, the ancient Bactria.
adj. Of or belonging to Bǒḵẖārā; — s.m. Native of Boḵẖārā.
s.m. Bark of a tree, &c. = baklā, q.v.
s.m. Pot-herb.
s.m. He-goat ( = bok; bakrā, qq.v.).
s.f. She-goat ( = bakrī, q.v.).
s.m. Gum-myrrh.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀩𑁄𑀮𑁆𑀮 (*bolla) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix)

def: s.m. Speech, speaking, word, talk, conversation, delivery, utterance, cry, voice; phrase, sentence; tune, air; words of a song; verse; taunt, jeer
subs: s.m. Making over one's share to another by word of mouth
s.f. The holder of another's share or inheritance; adopted heir
s.m. & adj. lit. 'High speech'; prosperity, success; — prosperous (a benediction used by faqīrs); — bol-bālā honā, v.n. To prosper
s.f. Talk, conversation, colloquy, confabulation; mode of speech, idiom, dialect; diction; words, dispute, altercation
(-kī), To speak, exchange words, converse together (after estrangement); to have words, to fall out, quarrel
v.n. To swallow (one's) words, keep quiet, be silent
(-kā), To taunt, ridicule; to disregard what is said, pretend not to hear (generally, in this sense, in connection with the words kān-meṅ; e.g. kān-meṅ bol mārnā).
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀩𑁄𑀮𑁆𑀮 (*bolla) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix)

def: s.m. Verbal agreement between two parties
subs: s.f. Talk, conversation = bol-ćāl, q.v.s.v. bol.

def: s.f. Speech, language, dialect; conversation, talk, saying; mode of speaking, idiom; bid (at an auction); song or note (of birds), chirrup; taunt, jeer; ridicule
subs: s.f. Reproach, blame; taunt, jeer; ridicule, raillery
(or simply bolī) pheṅknā, or sunānā, or karnā, or kasnā, or mārnā, v.n. To reproach, speak (at), jeer, taunt, laugh (at), ridicule, make fun (of).

ety: bol˚ = Prk. वोल्ल(इ), or लोल(इ); S. वद(ति), rt. वद्; or, prob. S. ब्रूय(ते), pass. of rt. ब्रू, corrupted to बूर्य(ते)
def: v.n. To speak, talk, tell, say, utter, pronounce, declare, give utterance to; to bid (at an auction); to cry (one's wares); to give forth sound, to sound, to bark (as a dog, &c.), chirp (as a bird, and the like, according to the animal); to strike (as a clock, &c.); to crack (with a sound), split, break; to be brisk or active (as a market)
subs: v.n. To leave word; to order (as goods, &c.)
v.n. To speak out, exclaim, cry out
v.n. To leave word (with, or at); to be proclaimed (on change), to be bankrupt; to be over, come to an end; to become; old or worn out, give signs of decay, &c.; to crack, split, break (as a vessel)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀩𑁄𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀇 (bŏllaï). Also compare بُلَانَا (bulānā, “to call”).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Saying; speaking, talking (e.g. boltī mainā, 'a talking mainā); — s.m. Faculty of speech; life, soul; a thing which sounds; pipe or ḥuqqa (in the language of faqīrs).
v.t. To sow, plant; to cultivate; to have (a cow or buffalo) bulled; — s.m. sowing; seed-time
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀯𑀤𑀺 (vavadi), from Sanskrit वपति (vapati).

def: s.m. = bauṅdā, q.v.
s.f. = bauṅdī, q.v.
s.m. Stack of straw; heap of chaff; a hollow bamboo; a drill; — the whole shell of a gourd, or of a cocoa-nut.
s.f. = bohnī, q.v.
s.f. Sowing; season of sowing, seed-time.
s.m. Stalk, stem; stump ( = būṅṭ); stopple; — finger, &c. = boṭ.
s.m. Stalk, stem; stump ( = būṅṭ); stopple; — finger, &c. = boṭ.
adj. Deep (syn. gambhīr).
s.m. Small bells for the pā-zeb or ornament worn on the feet (so called from their shape resembling the fruit of the jujube); studs of gold or silver; — vanity, conceit, pride
subs: (par), To be vain or conceited (of), to boast, make a display, show off.

def: s.m. Dip; fish-bait.
s.m. A gunny bag, a sack (syn. gon: cf. Lat. borassus).
s.m. A kind of bean, Dolichos catjang.
s.m. A kind of bean = borā, q.v.
s.f. A gunny bag; a bag in which bankers keep rupees; a measure of three maunds.
s.m. A mat made of palm leaves.
v.t. = boṛnā, q.v.; & = bolnā, q.v.

ety: boṛ˚ = Prk. वोडे(इ), or वोड(इ) = S. व्रोडय(ति), caus. of rt. व्रुड्
def: v.t. To dip, immerse, steep; to cause to dive, cause to sink; to drown.
s.m. Rainbow.
s.m. A kind of rice (which is cut in March or April).
s.f. — bursī, barosī; qq.v.
s.m. The reed of which writing-pens are made, Arundo karka; tube, pipe, conduit, canal.

ety: rt. of bosīdan; fr. S. चम्व, by transposn.
def: part. Kissing (used in comp.); — s.m. Kissing, kiss ( = bosa)
subs: s.m. Kissing and toying, dalliance ( = bosa-bāzī).

def: s.m. = بوسه bosa, q.v.
s.m. Kiss (syn. ćūmā)
subs: s.f. Kissing and toying, dalliance ( = bos o kinār)
(-ko), To give a kiss, to kiss
(-kā), To take a kiss (from), to kiss.

def: s.f. Kissing (used in comp., e.g. qadam-bosī, s.f. Feet-kissing; obeisance).

ety: perf. part. of bosīdan, 'to decay,' &c.; cf. S.व्यवसादित
def: part. adj. Decayed, dilapidated, worn out, old, rotten; stale.
s.f. Rottenness, decay, corruption.
s.f. Vessel; earthen vessel boat.
s.m. Finger; finger's breadth; digit; inch.
s.m. Tube of the nāryal, or huqqa, made of the cocoa-nut.
s.m. = بوته bota, q.v.
s.m. Young of a camel; a young camel; (met.) a fat person; an ungainly or ill-looking person.
s.m. A lump or piece of flesh; a log of wood.
s.f. Bottle: botal-ḵẖāna, s.m. Room where bottles, crockery, &c. are kept; pantry.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese botelha. Compare Konkani बोत्ल (botla).

def: s.m. Button.
s.f. A small bit, slice, or morsel of flesh or meat
subs: To bite or snap off a piece of flesh
boṭiyāṅ kāṭnā (-kī), To cut in pieces, make mince-meat of
prov. lit. 'With counted bits of meat (you will have) measured soup'; a limited income, small pay; — one's expenses must be in accordance with his income; cut your coat according to your cloth.

def: s.m. Buck-goat.
s.m. Sower of seed.
s.m. Jar (for pickles, &c.; syn. imrat-bān, martabān).
s.m. Land under cultivation; land not allowed to lie fallow.
s.m. Cream-coloured horse, a horse of a white colour tinged with red or brown.
s.m. = boza, q.v.
s.m. Monkey, ape (syn. bandar).
s.m. Brahmā or the Supreme Being regarded as a person, the first deity of the Hindū triad and the Creator of the World; — one whose calling and business consist in praying, priest, Brāhman
subs: s.m. Incarnation of Brahmā, appellative of a Brāhman
s.m. Grant or perquisite bestowed on a Brāhman.

def: s.m. The Supreme Being, the all pervading Spirit and Soul of the universe, the divine essence and source of all being from which all created things emanate and to which they return, the Self-existent, the Absolute, the Eternal; — spirit, soul; — sacred text, scripture (the Veda); — the class occupied with religious knowledge, the Brahmanical caste; a member of that caste, a Brāhman
subs: vulg. brahm-astra, s.m. 'Brahma's missile,' name of a fabled weapon (supposed to be the gift of Brahma) which deals infallible destruction to those against whom it is discharged (syn. brahm-bān)
(brahma+āsana), s.m. A particular posture suited to devout religious meditation
(brahma+aṇḍa), s.m. 'The egg of Brahma,' the mundane egg, the universe, globe, world; the top of the head, skull
(brahma+āvartta), s.m. 'The holy land,' epithet of the country situated between the rivers Sarasvatī and Dr̤ishadvati, to the north-west of Hastināpura or Dehli

s.m. A kind of vegetable poison; — name of a river rising in the eastern extremity of the Himalaya in Thibet, and falling with the Ganges into the Bay of Bengal; — a place of pilgrimage (probably the source of the river)
s.f. Epithet of the river Sarasvatī
s.m. The true knowledge of Brahma or the Supreme Spirit
s.m. Divine or spiritual birth, the second birth (effected by sacred study or knowledge), investiture with the sacred thread

s.f. (see next), A woman who leads a chaste and virtuous life; — brahmā-ćārī, s.m. A Brahman who practises continence or chastity, especially a religious student from the time of his investiture with the sacrificial thread till he marries and becomes a householder; a religious student who remains with his spiritual teacher studying the Vedas and observing the duties of a student; a particular class of ascetics; a Pandit learned in the Veda

vulg. brahm-ćarj, s.m. Sacred study, religious studentship, the condition of a young Brāhman or student in the first period of his life; religious self-restraint, pious austerity, continence, chastity
adj. & s.m. Imparting religious knowledge; — spiritual teacher
s.m. Curse of a Brāhman, anathema
s.m. The Mulberry tree, Morus Indicus
brahm-dait, brahm-rākshas, s.m. A Brāhman changed into a Daitya, ghost of a deceased Brāhman; apparition, ghost
s.f. 'Night of Brahma,' a period of time comprehending a thousand ages of the gods, or 216,000,000 ages of mortals
s.m. The aperture at the crown of the head through which the soul is said to escape on death
brahma-swarūp, brahm-sarūp, adj. Of the nature of spirit, of the same essence as the god-head
s.m. An edict addressed to the Brāhmāns; command of Brahmā, command of a Brāhman
s.m. Name of a sacred pool
(brahma-jṅa), s.m. One who has spiritual wisdom or who knows Brahma as the one all-pervading Spirit, a sage
(brahma+jṅāna), s.m. Divine knowledge, true knowledge of the Deity or of the Veda; spiritual wisdom
adj. & s.m. Possessing true knowledge of the Deity; — one who possesses spiritual knowledge
s.f. Murder of a Brahman ( = brahm-hatyā)
brahma-ghna, adj. & s.m. Slaying a Brāhman; — slayer or murderer of a Brāhman
s.m. 'Prayer-sound,' murmur arising from the recital of prayers; the sacred word, the text of the Veda; the reading or repeating of the Veda
s.m. The heaven or place of residence of Brahmā, a division of the universe and one of the supposed residences of pious spirits
adj. & s.m. Having the figure, or in the form, of Brahma; image of Brahma; appellative of a Brāhman
s.m. Extinction in Brahma, absorption into the Supreme Spirit
s.m. One who recites the Vedas, defender or expounder of the Veda; one who asserts that all things are Brahma, a follower of the Vedanta system of philosophy
s.f. Killing a Brahman, Brahmanicide; any crime equally heinous
(the preceding+S. kāra), s.m. (f. -ī), Slayer or murderer of a Brahman ( = brahma-ghātak)
s.m. A ceremony consisting in oblations to Brahma; — a free grant given to Brāhmans for religious purposes.

def: s.m. = brāhmaṇ, q.v.
subs: s.f. Distribution of food to Brāhmans.

def: s.m. A Brāhmaṇ, a member of the first Hindū class.
s.m. See s.v. brahma for all compounds beginning with brahma or brahm.
s.m. A young Brāhmaṇ.
s.m. = brāhmaneṭā, q.v.
s.f. A Brahmaness, — wife of a Brahman; a woman of the Brāhmaṇical caste.
adj. & s.f. = brāhmaṇī, q.v.
s.m. State, rank, or business of a Brāhmaṇ; the dignity of a Brāhmaṇ, Brāhmanhood, priesthood, priestly rank or character; an assembly of Brāhmans; — the planet Saturn.
s.m. = brāhmaṇya, q.v.
s.m. (f. brahmatā), State or condition of Brahma or the Supreme Spirit, identification with Brahma, godhead; office of the Brāhman or chief priest; state or condition of a Brāhman. Brahmanhood.
s.m. Worship or veneration paid to Brāhmans considered as one of the five great sacraments; — astonishment
subs: s.m. Dawn; the hour preceding sunrise.

def: s.m. Cattle-shed, cow-pen; — name of the district surrounding Agra and Mathurā (the scene of Kr̤ishṇa's juvenile adventures)
subs: s.m. An inhabitant of Braj; an armed attendant
braj-bhākhā, s.f. The dialect of Hindī spoken in the district of Braj.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Arabic بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Classical Syriac ܒܘܪܓܐ, borrowed from Latin burgus or Ancient Greek πύργος (púrgos).

def: s.m. See s.v. brahma.
adj. = bhrashṭa.

adj. & s.m. Engaged in religious ceremonies voluntarily undertaken; engaged in the observance of religious obligations or vows; pious; — one who fasts, does penance, &c.; ascetic, devotee, one who is under a vow; a religious student.
s.m. Tree (in general); plant (syn. peṛ).
s.m. Scorpion; the sign Scorpio ( = biććhū; syn. aqrab).
adj. & s.m. (f. ā), Old, aged, infirm; — aged person, old man ( = buḍḍhā)
subs: s.m. Old frame, infirm or weak body.

def: s.f. Increase, growth; vegetation.
s.m. The regent of the planet Jupiter and preceptor of the gods; (in Astron.) the planet Jupiter (syn. mushtarī); Thursday
s.f. Name of a particular metre of thirty-six syllables (divided into four verses of nine syllables each, or of eight syllables in the first, second, and fourth, and twelve in the third).
s.m. Wolf ( = bik).
s.m. = br̤ish, q.v.
s.m. = br̤ishabh, q.v.
s.m. Tree ( = birćh; brikh).
s.m. Heap, multitude, quantity, aggregation.
s.f. Sacred basil, Ocymum sanctum (syn. tulsī)
subs: vulg. bindrā-ban, s.m. A wood near the town Gokul in the district of Mathura, on the left bank of the Jamnā (celebrated as the place where Kr̤ishṇa in the character of Gopāla, or cowherd, passed his youth, associating with the cowherds and milkmaids employed in tending the cattle grazing in the the forest); a forest of tulsī trees.

def: s.f. Shame, modesty, bashfulness.
s.m. = br̤ish, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi बरस (barasa), from Prakrit 𑀯𑀭𑀺𑀲 (varisa), semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वर्ष (varṣá).

def: s.m. = birćhak; biććhū, q.q.v.
s.m. Bull; the sign Taurus.
s.m. Bull, ox, bullock ( = br̤ish).
s.m. = br̤ihaspati, q.v.

s.f. State, condition; means of subsistence, livelihood, allowance, stipend, pension, income, estate, property, endowment; a religious endowment or grant; behaviour, conduct; right, custom, privilege; — supporters, clients
subs: s.m. A kind of Hindī metre (consisting of four metrical lines or ćaraṇs, counted by syllables. It is of three kinds: 1˚ sam, or 'even,' having an equal number of syllables in each line; 2˚ ardh-sam, 'half even,' having an equal number of syllables in the first and third, and in the second and fourth lines; 3˚ bisham, 'odd,' having a different number of syllables in each line).

def: s.m. = br̤itā, q.v.
s.m. A religious endowment; a grant (generally of land) to a religious person, or to a tenant, on certain stipulations.
adj. & adv. Abortive, vain, useless worthless; in vain, uselessly, idly, needlessly, without proper cause, gratuitously; foolishly; — s.m. A useless or worthless thing; a nullity, dead letter.
s.m. Appointing; a proxy in the performance of religious duty.

s.m. Occurrence, incident, event; tidings, information, rumour, report, intelligence; tale, story, narrative, relation, history, account; topic, subject; mode, circumstance, manner, state, condition; sort, kind; difference
subs: s.m. The record of a decision given by a panćāyat.

def: s.f. = būʼā, and bū-bū, qq.v.
s.f. = būʼī, q.v.
s.m. Father; possessor, &c. (used in comp.; — before words defined by the Arabic article al, it is shortened in pronunciation to bu; e.g.); — buʼl-ʻajab, adj. Wonderful, marvellous
subs: adj. Given to excess, or to exaggeration; loquacious; prodigal
adj. & s.m. Desirous; whimsical, capricious, fickle; — a wavering, or fickle person; blockhead
s.m. Father dusty, a nickname given by Mohammad to his son-in-law ʻAlī
s.m. lit. 'Father of wailing'; the heron; the bittern
adj. & s.m. Of various hues, variegated, varied, various; changeable; wonderful; — anything of various hues; a chameleon.

ety: Pehl. boi; Zend baoidhī, rt. bud = S. budh
def: s.f. Odour, scent, smell; trace, soupçon, small portion, particle (in India, bo commonly signifies a bad smell, bū being used for 'smell' generally)
subs: (-kī), Smell (of a thing) to be diffused; soupçon, trace, or inkling (of an affair, &c.) to get abroad; to be divulged, to leak out, get wind
bo-dār, adj. & s.m. Scented, fragrant; — a kind of scented leather
adj. & s.m. Smelling, sniffing; — one who smells or inhales the scent or odour (of a thing)
s.f. Taking a smell; inhaling odour.

def: s.f. Sister, dear sister; father's sister, aunt (syn. phūphū; phūʼā; cf. bū-bū).
s.m. An old fool, old dotard; simpleton, blockhead ( = buṛbhas, q v.).
adj. Ancient, perpetual, everlasting.
s.f. Desire for food, hunger.
adj. Desirous of food, hungry.
s.f. Sister, elder sister; lady ( = bī-bī); favourite concubine, or one of superior rank.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Ear-cropped; without ears; — a man, or a dog, without ears.
v.n. To contract and put back (the ears, as a horse).

ety: S. अव or वि+कोचनीयं, rt. कुच्
def: v.n. To sit in a confined posture, to bend, squat down, crouch ( = dab-ke baiṭhnā).

ety: from būdan, 'to be'; Zend bū = S. bhū
def: s.f. Being, existence
subs: būd-o-bāsh, s.f. Existence; subsistence; residence, abode.

def: s.m. Wednesday = budh, q.v.
s.m. (?), Sound of water (cf. budbud).
v.n. To be; being, existence ( = būd, q.v.).
s.f. See s.v. būd.
s.m. Bubble ( = bulbulā: cf. next).

ety: S. बुदबुद, an onamatopoetic word imitative of the sound of a bubble rising to the surface of any fluid
def: v.n. To mutter, murmur, grumble; to speak inaudibly or indistinctly.
adj. = buḍḍhā and būṛhā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Old, aged; — old man ( = būḍhā or būṛhā).
s.m. Any wise or learned man, a sage; — the Buddha or 'enlightened,' who is regarded as the ninth incarnation of Vishṇu, and the apparent founder of Buddhism.
s.m. Old age, senility ( = buṛhāpā).
s.m. Old age, senility ( = buṛhāpā).
s.f. = buddhi, or buddh, q.v.
s.f. Perception, observation, intelligence, understanding, sense, intellect, mind, wisdom, judgment, discernment; comprehension, knowledge; thought, opinion, notion, reflection
subs: s.m. Strength of intellect; mental power
adj. & s.m. Of a deteriorated intellect; bereft of reason; — deterioration or destruction of intellect
s.m. Aberration of the mind or intellect
s.m. Name of Ganeś, as the god of wisdom
buddhi-rūpī, adj. & s.m. Of the manner or nature of intelligence; — the form or embodiment of intelligence
s.m. Ocean of intelligence (an epithet and a proper name)
v.n. To enter or be impressed on the mind; to be intelligible or comprehensible
adj. Devoid of intellect; senseless, ignorant, silly, simple, foolish
(buddhi+indriya), s.m. Organ of the intellect (as distinguished from an organ of action. Six are enumerated, viz. mind, eye, ear, nose, tongue, skin).

def: adj. & s.f. Old, aged (woman or female); — old woman ( = būṛhī).

adj. Endowed with understanding, intelligent, sagacious, sensible, wise, intellectual, learned; ingenious, skilful; — known, famed, celebrated; — buddhi-mān, budhi-mān, budh-mān, s.m. A man of understanding, shrewd or sensible man; wise or learned man, sage; — buddhi-matī, s.f. A woman of understanding, &c.

adj. Endowed with understanding, intelligent, sagacious, sensible, wise, intellectual, learned; ingenious, skilful; — known, famed, celebrated; — buddhi-mān, budhi-mān, budh-mān, s.m. A man of understanding, shrewd or sensible man; wise or learned man, sage; — buddhi-matī, s.f. A woman of understanding, &c.

adj. Endowed with understanding, intelligent, sagacious, sensible, wise, intellectual, learned; ingenious, skilful; — known, famed, celebrated; — buddhi-mān, budhi-mān, budh-mān, s.m. A man of understanding, shrewd or sensible man; wise or learned man, sage; — buddhi-matī, s.f. A woman of understanding, &c.

adj. Endowed with understanding, intelligent, sagacious, sensible, wise, intellectual, learned; ingenious, skilful; — known, famed, celebrated; — buddhi-mān, budhi-mān, budh-mān, s.m. A man of understanding, shrewd or sensible man; wise or learned man, sage; — buddhi-matī, s.f. A woman of understanding, &c.
s.m. See s.v. buddhi.
adj. & s.m. = buddhi-mān, q.v.
adj. = buddhi-mat, buddhi-matī, and buddhi-mant, q.v.
adj. = buddhi-mat, buddhi-matī, and buddhi-mant, q.v.
adj. = buddhi-mat, buddhi-matī, and buddhi-mant, q.v.
s.m. Stupid fellow, blockhead, fool, idiot (syn. bhoṅdū); — a proper name.
adj. & s.f. = buḍḍhī, q.v.
s.f. = buddhi, q.v.
s.m. A wise or learned man ( = buddha, q.v.); name of a son of Soma or the moon, and regent of the planet Mercury; the planet Mercury; Wednesday
subs: s.m. A wise or learned person.

def: adj. = buḍḍhā, and būṛhā, qq.v.
s.m. See s.v. budh.
s.m. The old bed of the Ganges, where it has shifted its stream.
s.m. = buḍh-gaṅgā, q.v.
part. Known, perceived, understood, comprehended.
adj. & s.f. Old, aged (woman or female); — old woman ( = būṛhī).
s.m. Wednesday; see s.v. budh.
s.f. = bū, bō, q.v. (būʼe is the usual form, necessarily, when the word governs a following subst. in the gen. case).
s.m. Breach (in a wall); hole, gap, fissure; — gaping would, gash
s.m. = بغاره bug̠āra, q.v.
s.m. Breach (in a wall); hole, gap, fissure; — gaping would, gash
s.m. A small bundle, &c. = buqća, q.v.
s.m. A cleaver used by cooks and butchers; chopping-knife.
s.m. A kind of camel (of high cost).
adj. & s.m. = bhuggā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of blunderbuss or musketoon.
s.m. Quadrangular sections of paste, macaroni; vermicelli (syn. siwaʼīṅ); an inflated substance resembling lights which protrudes from the mouth of a stallion camel in rut.
s.m. Malice, spite, animosity, resentment, grudge, hatred, rancour, malignity, revenge
subs: s.m. Unaccountable animosity; unreasonable enmity or spite.

def: s.m. Small sea; lake.
s.f. Sweepings; a duster.
s.m. = buhārū; — s.f. = buhārnī, qq.v.

ety: buhār˚ = Prk. वोहारे(इ), or वोहार(इ) = S. व्यवहारय(ति), rt. वि+अव+हृ
def: v.t. To sweep; to dust (syn. jhāṛnā; jhāṛū denā).
s.f. Instrument for sweeping, broom, besom.
s.m. One who sweeps, sweeper.
s.f. Fried or parched barley (syn bhūnā).
s.f. Goblin, bogie (a word used to frighten children).
s.m. The cover of a cup or other vessel; stopper, stopple; (cf. bujhnā, bujnā).
v.t. = bujhānā, q.v.
s.f. Perception understanding, intelligence, comprehension, thought (syn. samajh); guessing, guess
subs: s.m. A game at riddles; riddle, enigma ( = bujh-bujhauwal)

def: s.f. Guessing; a game among children (one of them is blindfolded and must guess which of the others touches him).
s.f. = bujhāʼī, q.v.

ety: rt. bujhā, of bujhānā+ī = S. यं
def: s.f. Explaining, instructing, teaching.
adj. Laden, &c. ( = bojhail, bojhal, q.q.v.); — s.m. Burden-bearer; beast of burden.
v.t. To cause to know, to make (one) understand or comprehend; to explain, inform, apprize, teach; to demonstrate; to cause to believe, to assure, persuade, convince, satisfy (syn. samjhānā).

v.t. To put out, extinguish (a fire, light, &c.); to allay, quench (thirst); to relieve, satisfy (hunger); to slake (lime); to temper (steel); to disinfect water (by plunging heated iron into it); to allay, calm, tranquillize, compose, soothe; to repress, smother (anger, &c.); to damp, depress, discourage (the spirits, heart, &c.); — to shuffle (playing cards
adj. See s.v. būjhnā.

ety: from bujhār-nā, an unused caus. = bujhānā+at = S. ति
def: s.f. Settlement or adjustment of accounts.
s.m. Guessing, conjecturing (e.g. phal-bujhauwal); riddle, conundrum ( = bujh-bujhauwal).
adj. (f. -ī), Extinguished, quenched, &c.
subs: s.m. Water that has been disinfected by having heated iron plunged into it

def: s.m. Riddle, enigma, conundrum; game of riddles ( = būjh-bujhāwal; syn. pahelī: cf. bujhauwal).
adv. Knowingly, wittingly, with premeditation, intentionally.
adv. Knowingly, wittingly, with premeditation, intentionally.

ety: bujh˚ = Prk. वोज्झे(इ) or वोज्झ(इ) = S. व्यवक्षाय(ति), rt. वि+अव+क्षै
def: v.n. To be put out, extinguished (a fire, light, &c.); to be quenched (as thirst); to be allayed or satisfied (as hunger, or a feeling or passion); to be slaked (as lime); to be tempered (as steel); to be disinfected (as water, by having hot iron put into it); to be calmed, cooled, composed, tranquillized, &c.; to be damped (as the spirits, courage, &c.), to be dejected, downcast; to be tired or weary (of, -se); to be shuffled (as cards): — bujh-jānā = bujhnā.
s.m. = bujnā (the com. form), q.v.
v.n. = bhujnā, q.v.

ety: būjh˚ = Prk. बुज्झ(इ) = S. बुध्य(ते), rt. बुध्
def: v.t. To understand, perceive, make out, comprehend; to conceive, think, consider, regard, account; to ascertain, inquire (of, se)
subs: v.n. To be understood, &c.; to appear or seem (to, -ko)
v.t. = būjhnā, q.v.; to come to an understanding (with, -se), settle accounts (with); to settle the amount of gain and loss (at cards, &c.)

def: s.m. Abstract account of a village proprietary.
v.n. = bujhnā, q.v.
s.m. Cloth used by a menstruous woman; pessary ( = bujhnā).
s.f. Tinsel; muslin.
s.m. Trumpet, clarion.
s.m. Land recovered by the recession of a river (cf. būkārā).
s.m. Place, house.
s.f. Weeping, lamentation, wailing, complaint.
s.m. Small pearl (cf. motī).
see būkā, and bokā.
s.m. Powder, any pulverized substance ( = buknī, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. bū.
s.m. Grinding, pulverizing; — powder ( = buknī).
s.m. Land recovered by the recession of a river, but rendered useless by a deposit of sand.
s.m. A small bundle (as of clothes, &c.), wallet, knapsack.
s.m. = بقچه buqća, q.v.
s.m. = بقچه buqća, q.v.
s.m. Fume, vapour, exhalation, steam, mist, fog; feverish heat, fever; warmth, anger, wrath, animosity; grief, anguish
subs: buḵẖār ćaṛhnā, v.n. To have a fever; to get into a state of feverish heat, excitement, or alarm (by reason of, -se), to be stricken with terror
To harbour, or cherish, wrath or animosity (against)
(-kā), To vent (one's) rage or spleen, to let off steam
(-kā), Steam to be let off; anger, &c. to be appeased; grief to be assuaged; heat of the system to find vent, or to break out (in an eruption).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُخَار (buxār), borrowed from Arabic بُخَار (buḵār).

def: s.m. Fumes, exhalations; vapour, steam, mist, fog.
(from A. buḵẖār), s.f. Orig. 'Stove or fire-place'; a hole or pit in a corner of a house to store grain in, granary.
s.m. Parsimony, stinginess, niggardliness, avarice
s.m. = buḵẖtī, q.v.
s.f. A Khorasānī or Bactrian camel; a quick-paced camel (generally used for riding).
s.m. Anything that makes a roaring noise; thunder; a roaring lion (cf. buḵẖt).
s.m. Handful, &c. = bukṭā, q.v.
s.f. = bukkī, q.v.

ety: S. अव, or वि+कर्तः, rt. कृत्
def: s.m. Tearing, scratching, clawing; claw, handful ( = bukkā)
subs: v.n. To fill the claw or hand (with); to scratch, claw ( = bakoṭnā); — bukṭā-bhar, s.m. Handful.

def: s.f. Cloth brought over the shoulders under the armpits and tied behind ( = bukkal, q.v.)
subs: v.n. To pass a cloth under the arm-pits and over the shoulders, and tie it behind.

def: s.f. Handful ( = bukkā; bukṭā); joining hands; friendship, intimacy.
v.n. To talk incoherently to oneself, to mutter, to rave; to talk or act foolishly.
adj. Dumb, mute.

ety: S. वृक्; or विकर्तन्, rt. कृत्
def: v.t. To pound, pulverize, &c. = būknā, q.v.

ety: S. वृक्; or अव+कर्तनीयं, rt. कृत्
def: v.t. To pound, pulverize, comminute, grind, reduce to powder, to powder.
v.t. (prov.), To eat, enjoy, take in or down ( = khānā; e.g. hawā būknā, 'to take the air,' = hawā khānā).
s.f. Powder ( = ćūran, ćūrā; būrā); small piece, shred, tatter: buknī kar-ḍālnā, v.t. To grind to powder, reduce to powder; to tear to pieces, shreds or tatters.
s.f. Powder = buknī, q.v.

ety: S. अव, or वि+कर्तः, rt. कृत्
def: s.m. Tearing, scratching, clawing; claw, handful ( = bukkā)
subs: v.n. To fill the claw or hand (with); to scratch, claw ( = bakoṭnā); — bukṭā-bhar, s.m. Handful.

def: s.m. Plants, herbs, pot-herbs.
v.t. To cause to be ground, pounded, &c., to have (a thing) pulverized or powdered.
s.f. Pudenda fœminœ ( = bur, q.v.).
wety: From Sanskrit बल (bala), from Proto-Indo-European *bel- (“power, strength”).

def: s.f. = H بلاون बुलावन bulāwan, s.m. = bulāwā, q.v.
adj. See s.v. A. bū.
s.m. The septum of the nose; an ornament worn suspended from the septum of the nose, generally set with a pearl or other gem.
s.m. The septum of the nose; an ornament worn suspended from the septum of the nose, generally set with a pearl or other gem.
v.t. See s.v. oulānā.
v.t. To call, send for, invite, bid, summon, cite; to challenge; — to cause to sound, to sound (as a ḥuqqā)
subs: v.t. To call, &c. = bulānā; (and intens) to call off or away.

def: adj. High, lofty, tall; elevated, exalted, sublime; loud (syn. ūṅćā)
subs: adj. Ambitious, aspiring, enterprising; large-minded, magnanimous
buland martaba, adj. Of high rank or dignity; high in rank, &c.
v.t. To lift, raise, raise up, elevate (syn. ūṅćā karnā, uṅćānā)
adj. Of lofty intention, high-minded, ambitious; — buland-himmatī, s.f. High-mindedness; magnanimity; ambition
v.n. To rise up, become high, ascend, mount, soar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُلند (buland). Compare Punjabi ਬੁਲੰਦ (buland) / بُلَند (buland), Gujarati બુલંદ (buland).

def: s.f. Height, elevation, loftiness, eminence, exaltation; pride; loudness; high land.
s.m. Calling, bidding; call, invitation, summons.
s.m. Calling, bidding; call, invitation, summons.
s.m. = H بلاهٿ बुलाहट bulāhaṭ, s.f. = bulāwā, q.v.
s.f. (In Persia and Arabia) the nightingale; and a certain melodious bird resembling the nightingale; (in India) the fork-tailed shrike, Lanius boulboul
subs: bulbul-ćashmī, s.m. lit. 'Bulbul-eyed'; a kind of silken cloth, having a cross-bar border with an eye or dot in each of the diamond squares.
ex:
سارے جہاں سے اچھا ہندوستاں ہَمارا
ہم بلبلیں ہیں اس کی، یہ گلستاں ہمارا
sāre jahā̃ se acchā hindostā̃ hamārā
ham bulbulẽ ha͠i is kī, ye gulistā̃ hamārā

Better than the whole world, is our Hindustan;
We are its nightingales, and it is our flower-garden.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلبل (bulbul), from Arabic بُلْبُل (bulbul).

def: s.m. Bubble; any frail thing ( = budbudā).
s.m. pl. of balad, q.v.
s.m. (?), Bulgaria; perfumed leather, Russia leather.
s.m. Bubble = bulbulā, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: s.m. (inf. n. of بلغ 'to reach'), = P بلوغت bulūg̠at, s.f. (for A. بلوغة), Maturity, puberty, full age, adolescence, manhood, attaining of a marriageable age.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُلُوغ (bulūġ), from Arabic بُلُوغ (bulūḡ).

def: s.f. (for A. بلوغة), = A بلوغ bulūg̠, s.m. (inf. n. of بلغ 'to reach'), Maturity, puberty, full age, adolescence, manhood, attaining of a marriageable age.
wety: From Arabic بُلُوغ (bulūḡ). Addition of ـت is likely from analogy with other nouns derived from Arabic, such as آخِرَت (āxirat), حِماقَت (himāqat), طَبِیعَت (tabī'at), عِبادَت ('ibādat), نَباتِیات (nabātiyāt) etc. Doublet of بُلُوغ (bulūġ).

def: s.f. The coming of age, adolescence, &c. ( = bulūg̠ai).
s.m. A large prominent eye.
v.t. To cause to call; to cause to be sent for; to send for, summon, &c. ( = bulānā)
subs: bulwā-lenā, v.t. To send for, &c. = bulā-bhejnā, &c., q.v.s.v. bulānā.

ety: Pehl. būm; Zend būmi; S. bhūmi
def: s.m. Land, ground, soil; region, country; — the owl.
wety: From Classical Persian بوم (būm, “earth, land”), from Middle Persian 𐭡𐭥𐭬 (būm), from Old Persian 𐏏 (būmiš), from Proto-Iranian *búHmiš, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *bʰúHmiš.

ety: Pehl. bun; Zend buna; S. बुध्न
def: s.f. Ground, foundation, basis, root, stem (cf. bunyād); bottom, end, tip.
s.m. Coffee, before it is roasted and ground (when ground it is called qahwa).
s.m. Foundation, &c. ( = bun); baggage, goods, household furniture (cf. bun-gāh).
s.m. The tip of the ear, the lobe of the ear.
s.m. The woof of lace.
s.f. = būṅd, q.v.
s.f. Drop; drop of rain; — blood; semen; — a kind of spotted silk
subs: excellent, fine, grand, superb (the word is used, in familiar phraseology, to serve as the standard of excellence, &c., and corresponds to our phrases 'the pink of,' 'the essence of')
adv. Drop by drop, by drops; every drop of, to the last drop
v.n. To secrete semen, to conceive (a woman)
adj. Rectified, doubly-distilled (spirit, sharāb), very strong (wine)
v.n. To appear like a speck (a kite); to rise to a great height, to be scarcely visible; (hence) to disappear.

def: s.m. Drop, &c. ( = bind; būṅd); ear-ring ( = bulāq).
s.m. A large drop: — būṅdā-tāi dī s.f. Small and interrupted dropping of rain; drizzle; distillation, filtration.
s.m. A kind of ornament worn on the ears.
s.m. Name of a spurious tribe of Rājpūts who give name to the province of Bundelkhanḍ.
s.f. A woman who sells perfumes.
s.f. Small drop; small rain, rain-drops; droppings; a kind of sweetmeat like drops; comfits ( = bundī).
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat (like drops); comfit.
s.f. A little drop, dot, spot, speck
s.f. Small drop; spot, speck, dot ( = bundkī, q.v.).
s.f. The rear of an army; baggage, equipage of an army.
v.t. See بننا bunnā.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: s.m. Sprig, stalk, stem; the plant of 'gram' or chick-pea (Cicer arietinum) with the green pods on it; the green pods of the chick-pea; chick-pea, gram (local); — adj. Small, diminutive, stumpy; compact and firm (as a pony, &c.; — cf. but)
subs: s.m. A dish made of green chick-pea and rice.

def: s.m. = būṭā, q.v.
s.f. = būṭī, q.v.; — name of a game among children.
s.f. Foundation, basis, base, origin, groundwork; resources, power (syn. aṣl)
subs: (-kī), To lay the foundation (of), to found; to originate, &c.

def: s.f. Foundation, &c. = bunyād, q.v.
s.f. A term of abuse applied to a woman, e.g. brimstone, strumpet, slut.
s.f. Chaff, husk; sawdust, &c. ( = būrā); rubbish, trash
subs: s.m. A sweetmeat (in the form of balls) made of the husk of grain; (fig.) one who holds out false hopes, or deludes or deceives; anything fair without and foul within, or that promises well but turns out ill; a well-looking person, or person of promising appearance, with nothing in him.

def: s.f. = bhūr, q.v.
s.m. (a term of abuse), Scoundrel, rascal, villain.

adj. (f. -ī), Bad, evil, ill, wicked, vicious, evil-minded, immoral, unprincipled, vile, base, villainous, mischievous, injurious, calamitous, destructive, baneful; ugly, ill-looking; disagreeable, unpleasant; odious, hateful; — s.m. Evil, ill, harm, injury; evil person
subs: s.m. A sect of Brahmans who perform the obsequies of the dead (syn. aćāraj)
v.t. To depreciate, represent as bad or worthless; to detract, defame, disparage
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Middling, tolerable, passable; — bad or evil person; reproach; abusive language; — burā-bhalā kahnā, To reproach, revile, abuse
s.m. Bad state; sad, deplorable, or wretched condition; — burā ḥāl karnā (-kā), To reduce to a bad or wretched condition; to ill-treat; to ruin
s.m. Any act forbidden by law; malpractice, vice; adultery, fornication (syn. bad-fěʻlī)
(-kā), To do wrong, harm, &c. (to), to wrong, harm, injure
(-ko), To speak ill (of), to pronounce or call (one) bad, evil, wicked, &c.; to vilify, abuse
v.t. To take ill or amiss, to be displeased or angry, to be affronted, feel oneself insulted
burā zamāna, s.m. Evil hour, evil time, evil days, hard times
s.f. Bad thing; bad language, abuse; bad conduct
burī gat, s.f. = burā ḥāl, q.v.
s.f.; — bure din, s.m. = burā waqt, q.v.

def: s.m. Powder ( = buknī); saw-dust, filings (particularly of wood); coarse sugar (syn. khāṅṛ); white sugar ( = ćīnī).
s.m. Redeemable mortgage.
s.m. = بورا burā; borā, qq.v.
adj. Old, dilapidated, broken; toothless.
s.m. Filings; sawdust; powder (syn. ćūrā).

s.f. Badness, evil, wickedness, vice, depravity, corruptness, vileness; mischievousness, hurtfulness, destructiveness; defect, harm, mischief: dissension; slander, calumny, detraction; evil consequences or results
subs: s.f. Good and evil; damage, mishap; reproach, censure, blame, accusation, &c. ( = ilzām)
burāʼī-par kamar baṅdhnā, v.n. To be bent on harm or mischief.

def: s.m. Bad man, bad character, villain; mischief-maker, slanderer; enemy.
s.m. Borax.
s.m. The mule on which Mohammad is said to have ascended one night from Jerusalem to heaven, and thence returned to Mecca; (met.) a swift or fleet steed.
s.f. Sound of anything falling or sinking in water
subs: s.f. Sound of water being poured out of a narrow-mouthed vessel; gurgling sound.

r: buraknā, per met., bukarnā, bukaṭnā, bakoṭnā
ety: per met. for bukarnā = bukaṭnā, not in use: cf. bakoṭnā
def: v.t. To sprinkle, strew ( = bhurbhurānā, q.v.).
s.f. Sound of a sinking body; a ball of lead for measuring the depth of water, sounding lead.
v.t. To cause to sink, &c. ( = ḍubānā, q.v.).
s.f. Food made from the egg-plant, fried brinjāl soaked in sour milk or tyre.
s.m. = burrās, q.v.
s.f. = buṛ-bhas, buṛh-bhas, q.v.
s.f. = buṛh-bhas, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = buṛ-bhasiyā, buṛh-bhasiyā.
s.m. = buṛh-bhasiyā, q.v.

ety: burā+bol, rt. of bolnā, q.v. + ā
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Foul-mouthed, abusive, scurrilous; — a foul-mouthed or scurrilous person ( = burā-bolnewālā).
s.m. Bubble ( = bulbulā, q.v.).
s.m. Bubble ( = bulbulā, q.v.).
v.t. = bhurbhurānā, and buraknā, q.q.v.
s.m. See s.v. buṛ.
